RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 206-C, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Rudy Veit, a Republican, represents parts of Cole and Miller counties (District 59) in the Missouri House of Representatives. He was elected to his first two-year term in November 2018.
In addition to his legislative duties, Veit is a practicing attorney at Carson & Coil Law Firm in Jefferson City.
Veit attends church at Saint Stanislaus and is a member of the Wardsville Lions Club, Jefferson City Park Foundation, Missouri Farm Bureau, Cattlemen's Association, Missouri Bar Association, Cole County Bar Association, Cole County Republicans, Lincoln University Blue Tigers Club, and the St. Thomas Knights of Columbus. He has served with the Jeff City Parks and Recreation Commission, Special Olympics Missouri, and the American Heart Association.
He holds a bachelor's degree in Agriculture Economics and a Juris Doctorate from the University of Missouri - Columbia.
Veit resides in Wardsville with his wife, Jeri. They have four children and six grandchildren.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies provisions relating to civil jurisprudence
Exempts corporations contracted with the state to provide dental care in correctional centers from the requirements for corporation licensure to practice dentistry
Modifies provisions related to workers' compensation administrative law judges
Repeals the expiration date of funding of basic civil legal services for certain persons
Grants circuit courts the ability to dissolve limited liability companies under certain conditions
Modifies provisions relating to the issuance and renewal of license plates and placards for persons with disabilities
Establishes the "Uniform Interstate Depositions and Discovery Act"
Creates the "Change of Venue for Capital Cases Fund" to reimburse a county that receives a capital case from another county for costs associated with the sequestering of jurors
Modifies the offense of tampering with electronic monitoring equipment
Modifies provisions relating to the compensation of jurors
Creates provisions relating to a battery recycling
Modifies provisions relating to medical testing for pediatric rare diseases
Provides for retention by the seller of certain state sales taxes due on transactions relating to entertainment
Reserves the House Chamber for the Association of Electric Cooperatives on July 17, 2025 from 2:00 p.m. to 4:00 p.m.
Grants use of the House Chamber to the Silver Haired Legislature on Wednesday, October 15, 2025

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Establishes provisions relating to electronic estate planning
